turns.user_message: I used to bounce back from little mistakes so easily, but lately I just feel completely stuck in my own head. It’s weird how quickly everything shifted, because just a few months ago I actually felt pretty solid about myself. Now I catch myself overthinking every single choice I make, wondering if I’m just slipping into some kind of rut. I keep trying to shake it off like I normally would, but the doubt just lingers longer each day. It’s frustrating to feel this disconnected from who I used to be.

This heavy feeling keeps creeping back in no matter how much I try to distract myself or push through it. I used to trust my instincts without a second thought, but now I second-guess everything I say or do. It’s exhausting to constantly monitor my own behavior, wondering if I’m coming across as completely clueless or just plain inadequate. The contrast between my old confidence and this new hesitation is honestly wearing me down. I feel like I’m slowly losing my footing in my own life, and it’s hard to pretend I’m fine.

I genuinely don’t recognize myself anymore because this constant wave of doubt has completely taken over my daily routine. It’s terrifying to think that the person who used to handle everything with such ease is just gone, replaced by someone who feels fundamentally broken. Every little setback feels like absolute proof that I’m just not cut out for anything anymore, and the weight of it is suffocating. I keep waiting for it to pass, but it just digs deeper into my head instead. I feel so hollowed out by my own perceived failures lately.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This just started recently.
